basics.name: Lucas Saunière
basics.summary: Doctor in Optics, Photonics & Image Processing. PhD thesis on modeling the instrumental response of the Euclid NISP instrument (DISPERS project, CPPM). Passionate about AI, computer vision, and machine learning applied to scientific instrumentation. Seeking postdoc in Canada or US.

work.company: CPPM — Centre de Physique des Particules de Marseille
work.summary: Modeling the instrumental response of the Euclid NISP instrument. Development of PSF modeling tools, Zernike polynomial decomposition, and deep learning methods for optical aberration estimation.
highlights: Developed the Spread framework for PSF-based aberration analysis
Published in SPIE proceedings and submitted to Elsevier Measurements
Presented at SPIE 2024 (Yokohama), Euclid Consortium Meetings

work.summary: Project: IASI-NG Interferometer aboard METOP-SG Satellite. Studied spectral calibration algorithm performance and validated calibration methods on IASI datasets.
highlights: Performance analysis of spectral calibration algorithms
Calculation of performance metrics on IASI predecessor datasets

work.summary: Project: Solar System Dynamics — Mars Ephemerides. Extraction and analysis of Mars Express/ExoMars position data; comparison with IMCCE simulations.
highlights: Data extraction from Mars Express and ExoMars missions
Statistical analysis of ephemeris accuracy vs temporal scales

work.company: IRAP — Institut de Recherche en Astrophysique et Planétologie
work.summary: Project: High-Resolution Spectropolarimetry of Beta Coronae Borealis with NEO-NARVAL. Analysis of stellar spectral data and determination of stellar magnetic field parameters.
highlights: Stellar parameter determination for Beta CrB
Theoretical estimation of magnetic field intensity

education.institution: Centre de Physique des Particules de Marseille (CPPM)
education.area: PhD in Optics, Optronics and Image Processing

education.institution: IPSA — Institut Polytechnique des Sciences Avancées
education.area: Master's Degree in Space Engineering (Space, Launchers and Satellites)

education.institution: MPSI/MP — Classes Préparatoires aux Grandes Écoles

publications.name: Spread: a framework for PSF-based aberration analysis. Estimation of Zernike coefficients and PSF reconstruction using deep learning
publications.summary: Authors: L. Sauniere, W. Gillard, J. Zoubian.

publications.name: Decoding optical aberrations of low-resolution instruments from PSFs: machine learning and Zernike polynomials perspectives
publications.summary: Authors: L. Sauniere, W. Gillard, J. Zoubian. Conference paper and poster.

publications.name: Euclid near infrared spectrometer and photometer instrument NISP in space
publications.summary: Authors: W. Gillard, S. Dusini, K. Jahnke, E. Prieto, N. Auricchio, R. Barbier, L. Sauniere, et al.

publications.name: Euclid commissioning results: the NISP signal detection chain
publications.summary: Authors: F. Cogato, E. Medinaceli, R. Barbier, S. Dusini, W. Gillard, L. Sauniere, J. Zoubian, et al.

presentations.name: Decoding optical aberrations of low-resolution instruments from PSFs

portfolio.name: DISPERS — PSF Modeling for Euclid NISP
portfolio.description: PhD project: modeling the instrumental response of the Euclid NISP instrument using Zernike polynomials and deep learning.

portfolio.name: Wine Aging Prediction with MLPs
portfolio.description: Applying multi-layer perceptrons to predict wine quality and aging potential from physicochemical properties.

portfolio.name: Computer Vision & AI Projects
portfolio.description: Collection of deep learning and computer vision experiments.
